scope: Viewport surface reconstruction only. The Wave/Bump layer appearance does not alter geometry and is not a slicer or G-code toolpath.

capture.lightScaleInterpretation: Blender Area energy and Three.js RectAreaLight intensity use different units. Each backend keeps the same normalized rig positions and sizes; the recorded scales align exposure rather than claiming equal physical power.

recoveredGraph.sourceColorAttribute: POINT-domain FLOAT_COLOR col, preserved as portable RGB
recoveredGraph.unassignedMaterialSlots: Null source slot remains null instead of shifting the later filament slot
recoveredGraph.frontColor: The authored col branch and Generated-coordinate Z Wave field
recoveredGraph.backColor: Geometry Backfacing selects the authored secondary Principled branch

interpretation: The browser now preserves the source FLOAT_COLOR col attribute, the empty first material slot, Blender's neutral unassigned surface, and the authored layer-line material instead of showing the former green diagnostic fallback. Exact topology, material-face counts, near-identical silhouette, and strong aligned luminance correlation are measured. Eevee and Three.js still differ in the high-frequency Bump response, so renderer identity is not claimed.
